code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Simple mapping for predicates to phrase templates.
    phrase_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of {obj}",
        "buildingStartDate": "construction started in {obj}",
        "completionDate": "was completed in {obj}",
        "floorCount": "has {obj} floors",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "cost": "costs {obj}",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of {obj}",
        "architect": "was designed by {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as {obj}",
        "height": "rises to {obj}",
        "buildingType": "is a {obj}",
        "developer": "was developed by {obj}",
        "tenant": "has a tenant {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses {obj}",
        "address": "has the address {obj}",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "bedCount": "has {obj} beds",
        "region": "is in the region of {obj}",
        "state": "is in the state of {obj}",
        "website": "has website {obj}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in {obj}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number {obj}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on {obj}",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building {obj}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {obj}",
        "leader": "leader is {obj}",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor {obj}",
        "governmentType": "has government type {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "currency": "uses currency {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in {obj}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {obj}",
        "numberOfRooms": "has {obj}",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {obj}",
        "architecture": "features architecture {obj}",
        "postalCode": "postal code is {obj}",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        parts = []
        handled = set()

        # Helper to clean quoted objects.
        def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
            return o.strip('"').strip("'")

        # Process each triple.
        for tr in group:
            if tr.predicate in phrase_map:
                phrase = phrase_map[tr.predicate].format(obj=clean_obj(tr.object))
                parts.append(phrase)
                handled.add(tr.predicate)

        # Add any predicates not covered by phrase_map.
        for tr in group:
            if tr.predicate in handled:
                continue
            parts.append(f"{tr.predicate} {clean_obj(tr.object)}")

        # Build sentence for this subject.
        if parts:
            sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts) + "."
        else:
            sentence = f"{subject}."

        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine sentences for multiple subjects.
    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    # Capitalize the first character.
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
